commit.message:
[Internal] Tests: Refactors StoreReaderTest to use async/await instead of sync-over-async (#5766)

## Description

> ��� **This PR was authored by GitHub Copilot** as part of an automated
sync-over-async cleanup workflow.

Eliminates all sync-over-async anti-patterns in \StoreReaderTest.cs\ by
converting 6 test methods from synchronous (\ oid\) to asynchronous
(\sync Task\) and replacing \.Result\ blocking calls with proper
\wait\.

---

## Changes

| Method | \.Result\ removed | Notes |
|--------|-------------------|-------|
| \AddressCacheMockTest\ | 1 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \TransportClientMockTest\ | 1 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \StoreReaderBarrierTest\ | 4 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \GlobalStrongConsistentWriteMockTest\ | 6 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \GlobalStrongConsistencyMockTest\ | 5 | \ oid\ \sync Task\,
\AggregateException\ \GoneException\ |
| \TestWhenNRegionSynchronousCommitEnabledThenDoBarrierHead\ | 4 | \
oid\ \sync Task\, \AggregateException\ \DocumentClientException\ |

**Total: 21 sync-over-async calls eliminated**

## Why
- \.Result\ blocks the calling thread and risks deadlocks under a
\SynchronizationContext\
- \.Result\ wraps exceptions in \AggregateException\, hiding the real
failure type
- \wait\ properly unwraps exceptions, so catch blocks now use the
actual exception type

## Testing
- All 6 tests pass locally (\dotnet test --filter StoreReaderTest\)
- No behavioral changes; only the async plumbing is updated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  - Air6208 info.json fields (core_addr, app_addr, etc.)

  - Frame sync on 0xA5 markers
  - Byte de-stuffing (0xA6 escape sequences)
  - 24-byte header parsing (ms, tag bitfield, cmd, sn, type, cpu)
  - CRC16-ModBus verification (init=0)
  - Printf format string + parameter reconstruction (%d,%s,%x,etc.)
  - Chinese UTF-8 text properly decoded

- CLI enhancements
  - flash run auto-detects chip type (bk72xx vs air6208)
  - flash partition commands chip-aware
  - log view-binary command for SOC binary protocol
  - Binary serial streaming in luatos-serial

- Tested live on COM7 with Air6208:
  - Full firmware flash (2MB, 1974 XMODEM blocks) ✓
  - Boot log decoding (27 entries with format string rendering) ✓
  - 34 unit tests passing ✓

commit.message:
fix(#132): set conversation title from first user message, overwrite with smart title after 2nd exchange (#133)

* fix(#132): set conversation title from first user message, overwrite with smart title after 2nd exchange

- Add titleIsPlaceholder flag to track whether the current title is a
  first-message placeholder rather than an AI-generated title
- After the first user message is saved, if the conversation is still
  untitled set a placeholder title from the first ≤40 chars of the
  message (with an ellipsis suffix when truncated) and persist it to
  Room so the conversation list updates immediately
- Broaden the smart-title trigger condition to also fire when a
  placeholder is present (titleIsPlaceholder == true), so the AI title
  still overwrites it after the 2nd exchange
- Relax the early-return guard in generateTitle() to allow overwriting a
  placeholder; clear titleIsPlaceholder once the real title is written
- Reset titleIsPlaceholder in startNewConversation() and on any manual
  renameConversation() call so neither path can accidentally overwrite
  a user-chosen title

- Convert 6 session child-table inserts to multi-row VALUES

fix: revert mid-loop OSR JIT execution (causes SIGSEGV)

The OSR path called try_execute_best_jit from within the loop body,
but Maglev re-runs from function entry reading globals from the
preheader. Mid-loop state (partially updated globals, stale register
file) causes invalid memory access. JIT compilation is still triggered
at the OSR thresholds; execution happens at function entry on the next
invocation.

commit.message:
perf: optimize secret-digger-claude token usage (#1894)

Address recommendations from #1889 (Claude token optimization report):

1. Disable GitHub MCP tools (github: false)
   - 52 unused GitHub MCP tools were loaded (~26K tokens/run)
   - Workflow only uses bash, cache-memory, and safe-outputs
   - Estimated savings: ~64% cost reduction ($0.51 → ~$0.18/run)

2. Add max-turns: 8
   - Claude engine supports max-turns (unlike Copilot)
   - Enforces hard turn budget to prevent runaway costs

3. Reduce timeout-minutes from 30 to 15
   - Typical runs complete in ~5 minutes with 3 turns
   - Halves the max cost ceiling for failure scenarios

4. Remove duplicate context from user message
   - Repository, Run ID, Workflow, Engine lines already injected
     by gh-aw framework into system context

Closes #1889

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[CORPUS] Manual PR ingestion: hydrator, normalizer, CLI command (#24)

- GitHubRestHydrator (IPullRequestHydrator): fetches PR details, files, review
  comments, commits, and diff concurrently via GitHub REST API; saves raw
  snapshots (pr.json, files.json, review-comments.json) to fixture raw/ dir;
  maps responses to domain models; uses GITHUB_TOKEN env var for auth
- GitHubApiModels: internal DTOs for GitHub REST API response deserialization
- ManualSeedProvider (IDiscoveryProvider): converts static PR URL list to
  PullRequestCandidates; GetProviderName=manual, no incremental sync
- FixtureNormalizer: deterministic HydratedPullRequest -> FixtureMetadata;
  infers tags (async, contract-change, early-return, null-safety, state-mutation,
  logging, exception-flow, api-change) from diff content; detects dominant
  language from changed files; defaults tier to Discovery
- CorpusCommand + add-pr subcommand: 'gauntletci corpus add-pr --url <url>'
  hydrates a PR, normalizes it, saves metadata.json + SQLite index entry, and
  prints fixture ID and tags; --db and --fixtures flags for path overrides
- GauntletCI.Cli now references GauntletCI.Corpus
- corpus subcommand registered in Program.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

commit.message:
fix(codegen): void ask fail-closed parity — panic via error slot for both local and remote paths (#938)

## Problem

Both void-ask lowering paths in ActorAskOpLowering silently swallowed
submission failures, while the corresponding non-void paths already
called hew_panic.

### Local void ask (hew_actor_ask)

hew_actor_ask returns null for BOTH a legitimate void reply and a
submission failure.  The old codegen only called free(null) and erased
the op, never consulting the thread-local error discriminant, so a
failed void ask silently continued.

Fix: call hew_actor_ask_take_last_error() immediately after the ask
and emit a conditional hew_panic branch when the error slot is non-zero.

### Remote void ask (hew_node_api_ask)

hew_node_api_ask has two execution paths:
- True-remote void success: returns a non-null internal sentinel.
- Local-delegation void success: PIDs with a local node-id route
  through hew_actor_ask_by_id, which returns null for void replies,
  and hew_node_api_ask returns that null upward unchanged.

A raw null-check (replyPtr == null → panic) is therefore wrong: it
false-panics on locally-registered actors looked up via Node::lookup.

The runtime bridges both paths into one node-level error slot:
hew_node_ask_take_last_error() returns 0 on success (regardless of
whether the return pointer is null or sentinel) and non-zero on any
failure.  Codegen must consult that slot rather than the pointer.

Fix: call hew_node_ask_take_last_error() immediately after the ask and
emit a conditional hew_panic branch when the error slot is non-zero.
No free is emitted (sentinel is non-owning; local-delegation null
requires no free either).

## Tests added

- test_local_actor_void_ask_panics_on_failed_ask: verifies
  hew_actor_ask_take_last_error is called, compared ne zero, and a
  conditional branch leads to hew_panic.

- test_remote_actor_void_ask_panics_on_failed_ask: verifies
  hew_node_ask_take_last_error is called (not a raw null ICmp against
  the ask return value), compared ne zero, a conditional branch leads
  to hew_panic, and no free is emitted on the reply pointer.

## Validation

  make test-cpp  → 122/122 tests passed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix menu bar % and popover % out of sync

Root causes:
1. ModelType.from() didn't recognize "MiniMax-M*" (minimax-m prefix)
   or "abab" series models as .text, so primaryQuota fell through to
   first-in-array rather than preferring the LLM model.
2. primaryUsagePercentage was captured from whichever model the API
   returned first, while primaryQuota preferred .text models — the two
   could pick different models and show different percentages.
3. StatusBarController used primaryQuota?.usagePercentage (a separate
   code path) instead of usagePercentage (same value the popover shows).

Fixes:
- Add minimax-m and abab prefix to .text classification
- After building quotas, re-derive primary model using same preference
  logic as primaryQuota (.text first, then first), so all primary fields
  are consistent
- StatusBarController now reads usageData.usagePercentage (same source
  as the popover) instead of primaryQuota?.usagePercentage

commit.message:
Fix Gemini lazy session reload

Keep bound Gemini sessions cold when reloading existing harness conversations. On first send, let the cold prompt fail, resume the bound session, suppress resume-time history noise until the ack, and retry the buffered message without touching server-side transcript hydration semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
